reviews.review_description:
A great puzzle game. Relaxing, fulfilling, enjoyable. 

My only complaint is that there are no more levels. Please please PLEASE make more! I’ll pay whatever you ask!

I still play it on “random” where I close my eyes & twirl the baton & randomly select one. 

But, come on, team…..MAKE MORE! :)

*I love the reactive piano notes, & how it’s a chord if you connect several nodes at once - brilliant<3

This game is AMAZING! The reactive music, the calm environment, the models you create… it’s all incredible! Genuinely one of my favorite games ever - I even played the whole game twice! The only thing that I think could be improved is the fact that there’s only 20 levels. Aside from that, this is AWESOME!

I completed this game and really hope that they come out with part two. It is almost meditative while you try to figure out the secret to creating the animals. ￼ Some of them can be quite challenging and is very rewarding when you complete them. I thoroughly enjoyed this game game. Give it a try, it really grows on you.

This game was really relaxing to just listen to music and complete little puzzles. It got pretty challenging towards the end which was a nice change. There should be more games like this, I definitely recommend. 👍❤️
This game is fun & relaxing. This is really the only puzzle game that holds my attention. I would like to purchase more games.
I want more and I will pay for it.

Skybinder is such a chill experience! I loved playing it from start to finish. Sure, there's room to spice up the celebration and menu, but it's the gameplay that kept me coming back every few nights to unwind. I finished every puzzle and it took some time. I would totally pay for more DLC levels. It's a fun puzzle game to relax with.

If I had any suggestions, it would be great to save mid-puzzle and maybe have a different solve screen to admire the creation a bit more before moving on. Definitely worth a download if you're into games like Puzzling Places. Keep up the awesome work!

I wrote a fairly positive review for this game recently, but after playing literally every night since I need to update it.  Skybinder is an AWESOME and ADDICTIVE game.  I liked it immediately, but only after doing new puzzles for a few nights did I realize how genius this title really is.  At first, I was fairly intimidated because the puzzles initially appear very complicated.  They ARE challenging, but once you understand the basics you'll be solving them without frustration and enjoying the trial & error.    All you're doing is connecting dots to ultimately reveal a 3-dimensional figure, but describing it really doesn't do the game justice.  I should note that Skybinder was initially offered as a paid app, but the developer seems to have decided to make it free and (perhaps) sell DLC expansions in the future.  I picked up Skybinder after it was free, and I am just shocked at how much content they're giving away.  I will absolutely buy an expansion pack DLC for Skybinder.  This is a really great, unique, and addictive game that you absolutely should try for yourself.  Get it (it's free and awesome)!

I love this game! I am always looking for relaxing games on Quest and they're so hard to find. From the moment I opened up SkyBinder, I was taken to a zen place. The puzzles are challenging and fun, and the audio is *chef's kiss*. Can't wait to recommend this to my friends.

This game is beyond description, utterly amazing, it's like someone took the concept of connect the dot style art AND tangrams, which I love, and made them 3D. Crazy fun and relaxing while also challenging your skill in spatial realtions. 

I saw it on a Oculus Facebook group and asked to be told when it was released as it looked amazing. Well whoever it was I asked promptly told me when it released and I bought it day one. I love supporting developers whenever possible, IF I like or believe in what they are doing and this game is beyond amazing and worth every penny.
I didn't know what to expect and I had a great time doing the puzzles. So addicting. I can't purchase anything in the app, however.

A cross between sudoku and dot-to-dot, Skybinder is a relaxing puzzle activity in VR, a game yes, but also a kind of meditation through activity - like a colouring book or jigsaw.

The game requires a mix of spatial reasoning, memory, and intuition as you mentally construct and reconstruct the possible shape in your mind.

The sound and visual design is satisfying and is clearly inspired by Cubism - which is a good thing!

3D puzzle games are what VR was made for imo. The more we support the more will be developed! (Surely we’ve had enough shooting games?)
i still enjoy connecting these dots into lines, and triangles, and little animals, even after a year of developing this game :) i hope you enjoy it too!

There is something beautiful when you get dropped into a game like Skybinder. The visual style, sublime sounds, minimalistic game play, and the way your brain engages with every brilliant and graceful puzzle is such a unique experience. There was something about each puzzle that created these zen moments where my brain was automatically and fully immersed in the game so much that before I was able to think what dots to connect next, my hands were moving, connecting dots, and creating triangles by themselves as the artistic animals came to life right in front of me. One of the best gaming experience and a must play.
This is a relaxing puzzle game where you connect the dots to make beautiful origami. The soundscape is really well done too.

I absolutely love this puzzle game. The concept of connecting the dots to make an origami-like creature is simple but the sound design and the way you can scale and rotate your model make for a richly immersive and pleasant experience. The sound and animation make you feel particularly euphoric when you complete a puzzle figure and you just want to keep connecting more dots!

Think Puzzling Places meets dot-to-dot meets origami. Wonderful game to get lost in, though I blitzed through it thinking there would be DLC packs but unfortunately not. I would absolutely buy more levels/models if they became available 🙏🏻
After few levels it is start to be bit boring.

This experience is something that is built for (literally but also figuratively) for the VR medium. After a quick and intuitive training, the player can easily stretch and rotate the world around them with a remarkable natural ease. This allows the immersion to really set in so that the music and the challenging puzzles come into focus. I really love this and could spend a lot of time in the experience, pushing myself cognitively while really vibing out!
